nodes.context: Italian criminal society originating in Sicily, mid-19th century, engaged in protection racketeering, drug trafficking, money laundering.
Offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia formed in the United States by Sicilian emigrants, replicating traditions and methods.
Mafia-type organization in Calabria, Southern Italy, distinct from Cosa Nostra.
Mafia-type organization in Campania, Southern Italy, distinct from Cosa Nostra.
Mafia-type organization in Apulia, Southern Italy, distinct from Cosa Nostra.
Mafia-type organization in Apulia, Southern Italy, distinct from Cosa Nostra.
Socialist peasant organizations in Sicily whose rise triggered landowners to turn to the Mafia for resistance.
American Mafia family led by Joseph Bonanno, who franchised heroin operations to Sicilian clans.
Sicilian Mafia clan involved in drug trafficking.
Sicilian Mafia clan involved in drug trafficking and the First Mafia War.
Coalition of Mafia clans from Corleone that dominated the Sicilian Mafia in the 1980s.
New York-based Mafia family with links to Cosa Nostra.
Sicilian Mafia family involved in drug trade and extortion, with alliances to other groups.
Mafia group in Corleone, Sicily, active in the late 19th century.

edges.context: The American Mafia is an offshoot created by Sicilian emigrants, replicating traditions and methods.
Cosa Nostra should not be confused with 'Ndrangheta, a separate mafia-type organization in Calabria.
Cosa Nostra should not be confused with Camorra, a separate mafia-type organization in Campania.
Cosa Nostra should not be confused with Sacra Corona Unita, a separate mafia-type organization in Apulia.
Cosa Nostra should not be confused with Società foggiana, a separate mafia-type organization in Apulia.
Landowners turned to the Mafia to resist and combat peasant demands from the socialist Peasant Fasci.
Joseph Bonanno franchised heroin operations to Sicilian clans and helped establish a Sicilian Mafia Commission.
Greco clan helped organize a drug shipment for the Sicilian Mafia in 1962.
La Barbera clan helped organize a drug shipment for the Sicilian Mafia in 1962.
La Barberas accused Grecos of embezzling heroin, leading to the First Mafia War.
Sicilian and Neapolitan crime bosses negotiated a joint monopoly over cigarette smuggling to Naples in the 1970s.
Corleonesi faction dominated the Sicilian Mafia Commission after the Second Mafia War.
Cosa Nostra yielded most of the illegal drug trade to 'Ndrangheta by the late 1990s.
News articles confirmed links between Cosa Nostra and New York's Gambino crime family in 2019.
Renzvillo family set up alliances with 'Ndrangheta according to 2017 investigation.
Renzvillo family set up alliances with Camorra according to 2017 investigation.
The American Mafia was created by Sicilian emigrants as an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia.
The Fratuzzi was a Mafia group in Corleone that was part of the Sicilian Mafia.
